incidents.activityLog.summary: Fetches a chronological log of all activities and status changes related to an incident, including when it started, escalated, was acknowledged, and resolved.

incidents.createComment.summary: Adds a comment to an existing incident for documentation or communication purposes. Comments help track incident resolution progress and team communication.

incidents.deleteComment.summary: Removes a specific comment from an incident. This action cannot be undone. Only the comment author or account administrators can delete comments.

incidents.get.summary: Fetches complete information about an individual incident by ID, including start/end times, duration, affected monitor, status changes, and resolution details.

incidents.list.summary: Retrieves a list of incidents (downtime events) from your UptimeRobot account. You can filter by monitor, date range, or search by monitor name. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

params.name: filter.monitor_name
params.description: Search incidents by monitor name (partial match)

params.name: filter.started_after
params.description: Filter incidents that started after this date (ISO 8601 format)

params.name: filter.started_before
params.description: Filter incidents that started before this date (ISO 8601 format)

incidents.listComments.summary: Fetches all comments posted on an incident, useful for tracking incident communication and updates. Results are paginated and can be limited.

params.name: filter.limit
params.description: Maximum number of comments to return (minimum 1)

incidents.sentAlerts.summary: Fetches information about all alerts (email, SMS, webhook, etc.) that were sent during an incident, including delivery status and timestamps.

incidents.updateComment.summary: Modifies the content of an existing incident comment. Only the comment author or account administrators can update comments. Changes are logged with timestamps.

integrations.create.summary: Creates a new third-party integration in your UptimeRobot account. Supports various integration types including Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, webhooks, and more. The integration will be active immediately after creation.

params.name: payload
params.description: Integration configuration object (see IntegrationPayload interface for all options)

integrations.delete.summary: Removes an integration completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. Any monitors using this integration will no longer send notifications through it.

integrations.get.summary: Fetches complete configuration and status information for an individual integration by ID. Returns all integration properties including type, URL, credentials, and current status.

integrations.list.summary: Retrieves a list of third-party integrations (Slack, Discord, PagerDuty, etc.) configured in your UptimeRobot account. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

integrations.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing integration. Only provided fields will be updated; omitted fields retain their current values. Changes take effect immediately for future notifications.

maintenanceWindows.create.summary: Creates a new maintenance window in your UptimeRobot account. During the scheduled maintenance period, affected monitors will not send alert notifications, preventing false alarms during planned downtime.

params.name: payload
params.description: Maintenance window configuration object (see MaintenanceWindowPayload interface for all options)

maintenanceWindows.delete.summary: Removes a maintenance window completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. If the maintenance window is currently active, it will be cancelled immediately.

maintenanceWindows.get.summary: Fetches complete information about an individual maintenance window by ID, including schedule, affected monitors, status, and configuration details.

maintenanceWindows.list.summary: Retrieves a list of maintenance windows from your UptimeRobot account. Maintenance windows allow you to schedule periods when monitors should not send alerts during planned downtime. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

maintenanceWindows.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing maintenance window. Only provided fields will be updated; omitted fields retain their current values. Changes take effect according to the new schedule.

params.name: payload
params.description: Maintenance window configuration updates (partial MaintenanceWindowPayload object)

monitorGroups.create.summary: Creates a new monitor group in your UptimeRobot account for organizing monitors into logical collections. Groups can be used for filtering, reporting, and managing monitors collectively.

params.name: payload
params.description: Monitor group configuration object (see MonitorGroupPayload interface for all options)

monitorGroups.delete.summary: Removes a monitor group completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. Monitors in the group will not be deleted, but will be removed from the group.

monitorGroups.get.summary: Fetches complete information about an individual monitor group by ID, including name, description, associated monitors, and group statistics.

monitorGroups.list.summary: Retrieves a list of monitor groups from your UptimeRobot account. Monitor groups help organize monitors into logical collections for easier management and reporting. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

monitorGroups.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing monitor group. Note: This endpoint only updates the name of the monitor group. Other properties cannot be changed after creation.

params.name: payload
params.description: Monitor group configuration updates (partial MonitorGroupPayload object)

monitors.bulk.pause.summary: Pause all monitors in the given monitor group.

monitors.bulk.start.summary: Start (resume) all paused monitors in the given monitor group.

monitors.bulk.update.summary: Apply shared setting updates to all monitors in the given monitor group.

monitors.create.summary: Creates a new monitoring endpoint in your UptimeRobot account. The monitor will begin checking immediately after creation. Supports HTTP, KEYWORD, PING, PORT, HEARTBEAT, DNS, and API monitor types.

params.name: payload
params.description: Monitor configuration object (see MonitorPayload interface for all options)

monitors.delete.summary: Removes a monitor completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. All historical data and statistics for the monitor will be lost.

monitors.get.summary: Fetches complete configuration and current status information for an individual monitor by ID. Returns all monitor properties including type, URL, intervals, and current status.

monitors.getAggregatedUptimeStatistics.summary: Fetches comprehensive uptime statistics for your account within a specified time frame. Supports predefined periods (DAY, WEEK, MONTH, etc.) or custom date ranges. Includes overall uptime percentage and optional detailed logs.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional parameters for customizing the response

params.name: filter.logLimit
params.description: Maximum number of log entries to return

params.name: filter.start
params.description: Start timestamp for CUSTOM timeFrame (Unix timestamp)

params.name: filter.end
params.description: End timestamp for CUSTOM timeFrame (Unix timestamp)

monitors.getFullList.summary: Retrieves all monitors from the account by automatically handling cursor-based pagination. This method repeatedly calls the API until all pages are retrieved. Useful for synchronization tasks where the complete state is required.

params.name: limit
params.description: Page size for each API request when paginating (max 200). Defaults to 50.

monitors.getFullList.example:
const allMonitors = await service.monitors.getFullList();
console.log(`Fetched ${allMonitors.length} monitors`);

monitors.getResponseTimeStatistics.summary: Fetches detailed response time metrics for an individual monitor within an optional date range. Returns average, minimum, and maximum response times, with optional time series data for graphing.

params.name: filter
params.description: Optional parameters for customizing the response

params.name: filter.includeTimeSeries
params.description: Whether to include detailed time series data

params.name: filter.region
params.description: Optional region code (`na`, `eu`, `as`, `oc`) or `all` per API; omit for default aggregation

monitors.getResponseTimeStatisticsByRegi.summary: Fetches response time metrics for a monitor **by region** (NA, EU, AS, OC) plus an `all` aggregate, for an optional date range. Defaults to the last 24 hours; maximum range is 90 days. Optionally includes time series per region block.

monitors.getUptimeStatistics.summary: Fetches detailed uptime statistics for an individual monitor within an optional date range. Returns uptime percentage, total checks, and downtime incidents for the specified period.

monitors.list.summary: Retrieves a list of monitors from your UptimeRobot account. You can optionally filter by status, group, or search by name/URL. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

params.name: filter.limit
params.description: Number of monitors to return (1-50, default: 50)

params.name: filter.name
params.description: Search monitors by friendly name (partial match)

params.name: returnFullResponse
params.description: If `true`, returns the full API response object (including pagination metadata). If `false` or omitted, returns just the array of Monitors.

monitors.pause.summary: Temporarily stops monitoring checks for the specified monitor without deleting it. The monitor can be resumed later using {@link Monitors.start}. Useful for maintenance windows or temporary service shutdowns.

monitors.reset.summary: Clears all historical data, statistics, and logs for the specified monitor while keeping the monitor configuration intact. The monitor continues running but starts fresh with no historical data.

monitors.start.summary: Starts monitoring checks for a previously paused monitor. The monitor will immediately begin checking according to its configured interval and settings.

monitors.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing monitor. Only provided fields will be updated; omitted fields retain their current values. The monitor continues running with the new configuration immediately.

psps.announcements.create.summary: Creates a new announcement that will be displayed on the specified public status page. Announcements can be used to communicate maintenance windows, incidents, or general updates to users.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

params.name: payload
params.description: Announcement configuration object (see PSPAnnouncementPayload interface for all options)

psps.announcements.get.summary: Fetches complete information about an individual announcement by ID, including title, message, status, publish dates, and visibility settings.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

psps.announcements.list.summary: Retrieves a list of announcements posted on a public status page. You can filter by status (offline, pending, published, archived) and paginate through results.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

psps.announcements.pin.summary: Pins a specific announcement to make it appear prominently at the top of the status page. Pinned announcements remain visible above other content to ensure important messages are seen first.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

psps.announcements.unpin.summary: Removes the pinned status from an announcement, allowing it to appear in normal chronological order with other announcements instead of being prominently displayed at the top.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

psps.announcements.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing announcement. Only provided fields will be updated; omitted fields retain their current values. Changes take effect immediately on the status page.

params.name: pspId
params.description: Public status page ID (number or string)

psps.create.summary: Creates a new public status page in your UptimeRobot account. The status page will be live immediately after creation.

params.name: payload
params.description: PSP configuration object (see PSPPayload interface for all options)

psps.delete.summary: Removes a public status page completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. The status page URL will become inaccessible immediately.

psps.get.summary: Fetches complete configuration and current information for an individual public status page by ID. Returns all PSP properties including friendly name, monitors, customization settings, and access details.

psps.list.summary: Retrieves a list of public status pages (PSPs) from your UptimeRobot account. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: options
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

psps.update.summary: Modifies the settings of an existing public status page. Only provided fields will be updated; omitted fields retain their current values. Changes take effect immediately on the live status page.

tags.delete.summary: Removes a custom tag completely from your UptimeRobot account. This action cannot be undone. The tag will be removed from all monitors that currently use it.

tags.list.summary: Retrieves a list of custom tags from your UptimeRobot account. Tags can be used to categorize and filter monitors for better organization and management. Results are paginated using cursor-based pagination.

params.name: filter
params.description: Query string parameters passed to the API

tools.buildUtcCalendarDayRanges.summary: Builds UTC calendar day ranges for a given number of days and a given timestamp.

params.name: dayCount
params.description: The number of days to build the ranges for.

params.name: nowMs
params.description: The timestamp to build the ranges for.

users.getAlertContacts.summary: Fetches a list of alert contacts (email, SMS, webhook, etc.) configured in the user's account. These contacts can be used for monitor notifications and alerts.

users.getAllAlertContacts.summary: Fetches detailed information about all alert contacts including their usage statistics, associated monitors, and configuration details. Provides more comprehensive data than {@link Users#getAlertContacts | getAlertContacts()}.

users.me.summary: Fetches detailed information about the authenticated user's UptimeRobot account, including email, plan details, monitor limits, and account status.
